Craft.Name: Salt-Thresh Weaving
Craft.DistinctiveProperty: Cloth woven with mineral-salt thread that resists rot and minor flame
Craft.MasterMarker: Can produce the double-salt knot without a loom guide
Craft.FailureCondition: active practitioners below 2 for more than one season

RootConflict.Adversary: House Thresker itself — internal succession dispute
RootConflict.AdversaryProject: Maren Thresker redirecting salt-weave materials to private trade while publicly claiming craft decline
RootConflict.SecretTruth: The craft is not dying; Maren has been diverting the family's salt stores to an outside merchant for three seasons
RootConflict.DisclosureStakes: Revealing the diversion restores the craft but exposes Maren to exile or worse

NpcRoles.Wants: For the double-salt knot to outlive the family name
NpcRoles.Fears: That the craft will be reduced to a trade good

NpcRoles.Wants: Had wanted to take the craft beyond the village
NpcRoles.Fears: Had feared becoming her mother's echo
NpcRoles.SelfDeception: Believed leaving would have saved the house

NpcRoles.Wants: To keep the family intact at any cost
NpcRoles.Fears: That confronting Maren will fracture what remains
NpcRoles.SelfDeception: Believes willful blindness is the same as patience
NpcRoles.ChangeCondition: Shown evidence of the diversion by someone outside the family

NpcRoles.Wants: To learn the double-salt knot before the knowledge vanishes
NpcRoles.Fears: That she is too late — that the craft is already hollow
NpcRoles.SelfDeception: Thinks persistence alone will compensate for missing instruction
NpcRoles.ChangeCondition: Learns that the salt stores still exist and were diverted, not spent

NpcRoles.Wants: Financial security outside the family's collapsing status
NpcRoles.Fears: Being trapped in a dying lineage
NpcRoles.SelfDeception: Tells herself the craft was already dying before she started diverting
NpcRoles.ChangeCondition: Confronted with proof AND offered a path that does not destroy her
NpcRoles.AntagonistVulnerability: Genuine grief over Senna's death; Senna was the only one who understood her
NpcRoles.AntagonistLineWontCross: Will not implicate Petra in the scheme
NpcRoles.ChangedByCondition: Player reveals Senna's journal entry about Maren's potential, then offers the merchant contact as a legitimate trade partner rather than a secret

NpcRoles.Wants: To tell the truth without losing his position in the house
NpcRoles.ChangeCondition: Player demonstrates that Corvin already suspects — removes Holt's fear of being the one to break the family

DangerousDisclosures.Condition: If maren_diverting is revealed to maren before InheritanceHinge resolves
DangerousDisclosures.Consequence: Maren destroys the salt stores, making Restored impossible

PressurePoints.UrgencyTrigger: Petra attempts a double-salt knot alone and ruins the remaining thread
PressurePoints.UrgencyEffect: Closes the Restored path; TransformedA becomes the best achievable

Paths.EmotionalCostDescription: Corvin must admit he was pretending not to know
Family accepts outside help — seen as admitting defeat

PressurePoints.UrgencyTrigger: Maren notices Holt speaking privately with the player
PressurePoints.UrgencyEffect: Holt leaves the village; WitnessFact salt_stores_intact becomes unavailable from Holt

TransitionRules.Condition: Player learns salt_stores_intact from any source
UrgencyTrigger fires before player speaks to Holt

Paths.EmotionalCostDescription: Holt risks Maren's anger and his place in the house
Player finds the ledger without Holt — he is never acknowledged

PressurePoints.UrgencyTrigger: Corvin makes Maren interim head without confrontation
PressurePoints.UrgencyEffect: Closes Restored; opens Corrupted path weight +2

TransitionRules.Condition: Player speaks to Corvin after salt_stores_intact is known

Paths.EmotionalCostDescription: Corvin breaks his self-deception; family fractures briefly before healing
Maren is allowed to stay if she stops diverting — silence becomes the price of peace
Maren is exiled; the house is clean but diminished

PressurePoints.UrgencyTrigger: None — evidence is static unless Maren is warned
PressurePoints.UrgencyEffect: If maren_diverting revealed to Maren early, she burns the ledger

TransitionRules.Condition: Maren warned before ledger is found

HiddenEvidence.GateCondition: Locked in Maren's workroom; key held by Maren or obtainable from Holt

RevealMethods.Method: Ask Holt for the key after he confides
Find the ledger through the loose hearthstone Senna's journal describes

Paths.EmotionalCostDescription: Reading Senna's private accounts feels like trespass
Evidence is gone; only testimony remains

Interactions.Description: Oil the loom frame with salt-water as Aldric taught — it preserves the wood
Leave the salt pouch open so the moisture evaporates — let it complete its cycle
Fold the cloak and return it to the house — she belongs here
Hang the cloak at the crossroads — where she was going, not where she stopped

EndStates.Name: The Knot Holds
EndStates.ExternalResonanceDescription: Salt-weave cloth reappears at the village market; other houses note House Thresker's recovery

EndStates.Name: New Fiber
EndStates.ExternalResonanceDescription: The craft survives but changes hands in spirit — outsiders now shape it

EndStates.Name: The Comfortable Lie
EndStates.ExternalResonanceDescription: House Thresker appears stable; those who know say nothing

EndStates.Name: Thread's End
EndStates.ExternalResonanceDescription: House Thresker dissolves; the double-salt knot is lost

EndStates.Name: Salt in the Wound
EndStates.ExternalResonanceDescription: The craft becomes a private commercial operation; salt-weave loses its distinctiveness within two seasons

CorruptionGradient.EarlyWarningSignal: Maren begins pricing salt-weave samples with the merchant's marks visible on the backing
CorruptionGradient.PointOfNoReturn: SilentBargain path taken in InheritanceHinge while LedgerBurned is already resolved
